onboarding.question:
1. [Design] Screen 1j (Chat Home Empty State — Logged-In) does not define tablet layout behavior; only mobile and desktop form factors are implied by inheritance from Screen 1a, which itself lacks an explicit tablet layout definition. — Add an explicit tablet (640px–1023px) layout entry to both Screen 1a and Screen 1j specifying max-width 760px centered with margin: 0 auto, matching the conversation area max-width defined in Screen
1.
2. [Design] Screen 5 (Top Navigation Bar) Account Menu logged-in state does not specify what menu items appear beyond "Sign out" — no explicit complete list of menu items is provided for either mobile or web layout. — Add a complete exhaustive list of all menu items (with exact label text) to the Account Menu section; if "Sign out" is the only item, state explicitly "Menu contains exactly one item: 'Sign out'."
3. [Design] Screen 5 (Top Navigation Bar) loading state does not define layout for tablet viewport (640px–1023px); only "Mobile & Web" is referenced generically without breakpoint-specific treatment. — Add an explicit tablet (640px–1023px) layout entry to the Loading State section of Screen 5 specifying height (56px or 64px), padding, and skeleton placeholder positioning.
4. [Design] Screen 7 (Starter Chips) tablet layout (640px–1023px) is not defined — only mobile and desktop max-widths are inferable; the spec does not explicitly address tablet chip row max-width or centering behavior. — Add an explicit tablet (640px–1023px) layout entry to Screen 7 specifying max-width 760px centered with margin: 0 auto.
5. [Design] The skeleton shimmer animation in the Design System Loading State section references colors "rgba(248,248,247,0.08)" which is not a named brand token. — Replace "rgba(248,248,247,0.08)" with a named token; add "--shimmer-highlight: rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.08)" to the Brand token table and reference it by token name.
6. [Design] Screen 5 (Top Navigation Bar) loading state does not define the shimmer animation duration, easing, or direction for the skeleton placeholder, only referencing "linear-gradient shimmer" without timing details. — Specify the skeleton shimmer animation in Screen 5 loading state as: linear-gradient sweep from `--surface` to `--shimmer-highlight` to `--surface`, animated over 1.5s linear infinite, traveling left to right — matching the Design System Loading State definition.
7. [Design] Screen 4 (In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge) specifies that the nudge "appears with no entry animation — it renders instantly" but the nudge heading uses `--violet` as a color value; `--violet` is defined as #7C6FCD in the Brand section, which is consistent, so this is not a conflict — however, the button hover state is described only as "0.20s transition on opacity" without specifying the start and end opacity values. — Define the button hover opacity transition explicitly as: opacity transitions from 1.0 to 0.9 over 200ms ease-out on hover entry, and from 0.9 to 1.0 over 200ms ease-out on hover exit.
8. [Design] Screen 1f (Session Expiry Warning) specifies the warning text as hardcoded "10 minutes" but does not define behavior if the timer warning fires outside the 10-minute window due to clock drift or retry — however more critically, the spec does not define what happens visually if the user resets the inactivity timer and the banner clears but the session TTL is still below 10 minutes remaining (i.e., does the banner reappear on the next inactivity threshold crossing within the same session). — Add an explicit behavioral rule stating: "The session expiry warning banner fires exactly once per logged-out session; after it clears due to inactivity timer reset, it does not reappear even if the user becomes inactive again within the same session."
9. [Design] Screen 1e (Session Expired Error State) describes the chat interface as "remains interactive but unresponsive" which is vague language two engineers could interpret differently (interactive implies inputs are enabled; unresponsive implies they are not). — Replace "remains interactive but unresponsive" with: "Chat input field and send button remain visually enabled (no opacity change, pointer-events active) but submitting a message]
